criterion: The Ports UI renders the host's listening ports (the live ss scan).
observed: Header count '51 listening · 32 exposed · 19 loopback' highlighted above a table of real host listeners (22, 53, 443, 631, 3702, 3733, 5903, 7077-7089, ...) with Port/Bind/Label/PID/Actions columns.

criterion: A Garrison-owned port is LABELED from its ui-fittings status file (shows its name, not just a pid).
observed: Row 46001 (0.0.0.0) Label cell highlighted reading 'demo-fitting' with a 'fitting' source chip; the pid (3328070) sits separately in the PID column. Labeled from the seeded ui-fittings/demo-fitting.json, not the bare process name.

criterion: Loopback-only listeners carry the loopback badge; tailnet-reachable rows offer an open-URL action while loopback rows hide it.
observed: demo-local (46002, 127.0.0.1) shows a highlighted 'loopback' chip and NO Open link (only Browser pane / Copy URL / Kill). demo-fitting (46001, 0.0.0.0, 'all interfaces') directly above DOES show an 'Open' link. Contrast confirmed.

criterion: Copy URL action present on rows.
observed: Row 46001 Actions column shows a highlighted 'Copy URL' button alongside Open / Browser pane / Kill.

criterion: Kill action: SIGTERM with confirm, demonstrated only on our own sacrificial server; row gone after rescan.
assert: .banner-notice contains "Sent SIGTERM" (highlighted); tr:has-text("demo-fitting") reached state=detached before the assert
observed: Clicked Kill on demo-fitting (pid 3328070), then the in-app 'Confirm SIGTERM' button (not a native dialog); highlighted notice 'Sent SIGTERM to pid 3328070'. The row detached from the DOM (functional truth) and the header count dropped 51->50 listening / 32->31 exposed, confirming the exposed demo-fitting was killed and the rescan dropped it. Only our own server was targeted.
